text.title: TruffleHog Verified-Only CI Gate
text.summary: TruffleHog runs in CI with `--results=verified` so only secrets that the issuer confirms as live trigger the merge block.

section.title: Verification removes false positives
section.p: Pattern matchers cannot tell a real production token from one in an example. TruffleHog calls the issuer (AWS STS, GitHub, Stripe, OpenAI, Anthropic, Slack) to confirm the candidate is live; verified findings are by definition exploitable.

rule.statement: Every PR MUST run TruffleHog against the diff between `base..head` with `--results=verified` and the job MUST fail the required check on any verified finding. Unverified findings are reported as warnings, not blockers, to keep the gate signal clean. The PR description MUST link the rotation receipt before merge if a verified finding is acknowledged as expected (rare — typically only test fixtures using known invalidated keys).
rule.rationale: A verified finding is not a "potential secret" — it is a key that the issuer just answered as authentic. Treating it as anything other than P0 is a process bug. Restricting the merge gate to verified findings stops alert fatigue and gives agents a sharp signal they can act on.

example.note: Diff-scoped, verified-only; reproducible signal for the required check.
Whole-tree scan with no verification floods the queue with false positives; team disables the gate.

antipattern.description: Running TruffleHog non-verified and adding everything it finds to the dismiss list.
antipattern.reason: The dismiss list grows until real secrets land and are auto-suppressed; pairing diff-scope with `--results=verified` gives you a list that stays small and meaningful.
